prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the highest single-game score in NBA history? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: 100 points -- Wilt Chamberlain on March 2, 1962 (no video exists of the full game)

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What does 'Mamba Mentality' refer to and who coined it? Winner gives out 2 sips.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What year did LeBron James make 'The Decision' to leave Cleveland for Miami on live TV? Winner gives out 3 sips.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: Stephen Curry holds the record for most 3-pointers made in NBA history. Approximately how many? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Over 3,700+ (and counting) -- he's in a league of his own for 3-point shooting

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What NBA team blew a 3-1 lead in the 2016 Finals, the only team to do so? Winner gives out 3 sips.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the name of the NBA's annual slam dunk competition? Winner gives out 2 sips.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: Who won the first NBA MVP award? Winner gives out 4 sips.
prompts.answer: Bob Pettit of the St. Louis Hawks (1956)

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What NBA team plays in 'The Garden'? Winner gives out 2 sips.

prompts.answer: Tom Brady -- 7 Super Bowl rings

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the only NFL team to go undefeated for an entire season including the Super Bowl? Winner gives out 3 sips.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the largest comeback in Super Bowl history? Winner gives out 3 sips.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What NFL player is known as 'The GOAT' and played until age 45? Winner gives out 2 sips.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the 'Immaculate Reception' in NFL history? Winner gives out 3 sips.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: How long is an NFL football field in yards (end zone to end zone)? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: 120 yards total (100 yards of playing field + two 10-yard end zones)

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What NFL team went 0-16 in 2008? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The Detroit Lions -- the first team to lose every game in a 16-game season

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the Lombardi Trophy? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: The trophy awarded to the Super Bowl winner -- named after legendary coach Vince Lombardi

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the NFL Draft's first pick called? Winner gives out 2 sips.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What quarterback is famous for 'Omaha!' audible calls? Winner gives out 2 sips.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: In what year was the first Super Bowl played? Winner gives out 3 sips.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What does CTE stand for, the brain disease linked to football? Winner gives out 3 sips.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: Who has won the most Ballon d'Or awards? Winner gives out 2 sips.

First to shout it out: What happened in the 'Hand of God' goal? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Diego Maradona punched the ball into the net with his fist against England in the 1986 World Cup quarterfinal -- the ref didn't see it and counted it as a goal

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the most-watched sporting event in the world? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: The FIFA World Cup Final -- over 1.5 billion viewers for the 2022 final

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What club has Cristiano Ronaldo NOT played for: Man United, Barcelona, Real Madrid, or Juventus? Winner gives out 3 sips.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: Who scored the winning penalty in the 2022 World Cup Final for Argentina? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Gonzalo Montiel -- in the penalty shootout against France. Messi scored in the shootout too, but Montiel scored the winner.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is an 'own goal' in soccer? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: When a player accidentally scores in their own team's net -- it counts as a goal for the opposing team

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is 'added time' or 'stoppage time' in soccer? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Extra time added to the end of each half to make up for stoppages (injuries, substitutions, time-wasting). The referee decides how much.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What soccer club is nicknamed 'The Red Devils'? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: Manchester United (also Belgium's national team)

prompts.text: First to shout it out: Who is the all-time top scorer in men's international soccer? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Cristiano Ronaldo -- 130+ international goals for Portugal

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the 'offside rule' in simple terms? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: A player can't be behind the last defender when the ball is passed to them -- they must be level or behind the ball

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is VAR in soccer? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: Video Assistant Referee -- technology used to review controversial decisions

prompts.text: First to shout it out: Who holds the record for most career home runs in MLB? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: Barry Bonds -- 762 home runs (though his record is controversial due to steroids)

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What curse supposedly kept the Boston Red Sox from winning the World Series for 86 years? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The Curse of the Bambino -- after trading Babe Ruth to the Yankees in 1920. They finally won in 2004.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What does 'ERA' stand for in baseball? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: Earned Run Average -- the average number of earned runs a pitcher allows per 9 innings

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What MLB player was the first to break the color barrier in 1947? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: Jackie Robinson -- playing for the Brooklyn Dodgers. His #42 is retired across all of MLB.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the 'seventh-inning stretch' in baseball? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: A tradition where fans stand up and stretch during the middle of the 7th inning -- often singing 'Take Me Out to the Ball Game'

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What does a 'no-hitter' mean in baseball? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: A game where a pitcher (or pitching staff) doesn't allow any hits -- batters can still reach base via walks, errors, or hit-by-pitch

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What Japanese player became the first two-way star in modern MLB, pitching and hitting? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: Shohei Ohtani -- compared to Babe Ruth for excelling at both pitching and hitting

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What MLB team has won the most World Series titles? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: The New York Yankees -- 27 World Series titles

prompts.text: First to shout it out: In what year did the 'Moneyball' Oakland A's have their famous 20-game winning streak? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: 2002 -- featured in the book/movie Moneyball starring Brad Pitt

prompts.text: First to shout it out: Who is the most famous UFC fighter of all time, known for his left hand and trash talk? Winner gives out 2 sips.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What does UFC stand for? Winner gives out 2 sips.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What weight class does the UFC heavyweight division max out at? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: 265 pounds (120 kg) -- anything above that can't compete

prompts.text: First to shout it out: Who holds the record for most UFC title defenses? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Demetrious Johnson -- 11 consecutive flyweight title defenses

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What fighter is known as 'The Spider' and held the middleweight title for 2,457 days? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Anderson Silva -- one of the longest title reigns in UFC history

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What does a 'rear naked choke' do in MMA? Winner gives out 3 sips.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What famous UFC moment involved Conor McGregor and a bus? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: McGregor threw a hand truck at a bus carrying rival fighters at UFC 223 media day (2018) -- he was arrested

prompts.text: First to shout it out: Who did Ronda Rousey lose to that ended her undefeated streak? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Holly Holm -- knocked her out with a head kick at UFC 193 (2015)

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What Khabib Nurmagomedov record stands out the most? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: 29-0 -- he retired undefeated, one of the greatest MMA records ever

prompts.text: First to shout it out: Who holds the record for most Formula 1 World Championships? Winner gives out 2 sips.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the most famous F1 race in the world, held on the streets of a tiny country? Winner gives out 2 sips.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What does DRS stand for in Formula 1? Winner gives out 3 sips.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What Netflix series made Formula 1 popular with new fans? Winner gives out 2 sips.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What F1 team is known for their red cars and Italian heritage? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: Scuderia Ferrari

prompts.text: First to shout it out: Who won the controversial 2021 F1 Championship on the last lap of the last race? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Max Verstappen -- the race director's safety car restart decision is still debated. Lewis Hamilton was leading.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What speed can an F1 car reach? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Over 230 mph (370 km/h) on straights. They can go 0-60 in about 2.6 seconds.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: In F1, what is a 'pit stop' and how fast are they? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Stopping to change tires/make adjustments. The fastest pit stops are under 2 seconds for all 4 tires.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: Who is the most decorated Olympian of all time? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: Michael Phelps -- 28 Olympic medals (23 gold) in swimming

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What city hosted the first modern Olympic Games? Winner gives out 3 sips.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What do the 5 Olympic rings represent? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: The five continents of the world united by the Olympics (Africa, Americas, Asia, Europe, Oceania)

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What sprinter holds the world record for the 100-meter dash? Winner gives out 2 sips.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What country has won the most Olympic gold medals in history? Winner gives out 2 sips.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What gymnast is considered the GOAT and has skills named after her? Winner gives out 2 sips.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the Olympic motto? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: 'Citius, Altius, Fortius -- Communiter' (Faster, Higher, Stronger -- Together). 'Together' was added in 2021.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What happened at the 1972 Munich Olympics that changed security forever? Winner gives out 3 sips.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: Usain Bolt ate almost exclusively what food during the 2008 Beijing Olympics where he won 3 golds? Winner gives out 4 sips.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What does an athlete get for winning Olympic gold, silver, and bronze? Winner gives out 2 sips.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: Who holds the record for most goals in NHL history? Winner gives out 2 sips.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the Stanley Cup? Winner gives out 2 sips.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is a 'hat trick' in hockey? Winner gives out 2 sips.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: Why do hockey players lose their teeth? Winner gives out 3 sips.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What NHL team has won the most Stanley Cups? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The Montreal Canadiens -- 24 Stanley Cup championships

prompts.text: First to shout it out: Who has the most Grand Slam singles titles in men's tennis? Winner gives out 2 sips.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What surface is Wimbledon played on? Winner gives out 2 sips.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What tennis player has 23 Grand Slam titles and a fashion line? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: Serena Williams

prompts.text: First to shout it out: In tennis, what is a score of 40-40 called? Winner gives out 2 sips.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What golfer is considered the greatest of all time with 15 major titles? Winner gives out 2 sips.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What color jacket does the Masters winner receive? Winner gives out 2 sips.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is a 'hole in one' on a par 3? Winner gives out 2 sips.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: Who is known as 'The Greatest' in boxing? Winner gives out 2 sips.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What was the 'Rumble in the Jungle'? Winner gives out 3 sips.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is Floyd Mayweather's professional boxing record? Winner gives out 3 sips.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What sport uses the terms 'love,' 'deuce,' and 'ace'? Winner gives out 2 sips.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What sport is played on the largest field/pitch? Winner gives out 3 sips.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the oldest sport still played today? Winner gives out 3 sips.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: In what sport can you score a 'try'? Winner gives out 3 sips.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What sport is the most popular in India? Winner gives out 2 sips.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What does FIFA stand for? Winner gives out 3 sips.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: How long is a marathon in miles? Winner gives out 2 sips.
First to shout it out: Why is a marathon 26.2 miles and not a round number? Winner gives out 4 sips.
prompts.answer: The 1908 London Olympics added extra distance so the race could finish in front of the Royal Box at the Olympic Stadium. That distance stuck.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What sport has a scoring system that goes 15, 30, 40, game? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: Tennis -- and nobody fully agrees on why those numbers are used

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the highest-paid sport in the world by average salary? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: NBA basketball -- average salary is about $10 million per year

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What country invented basketball? Winner gives out 3 sips.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is a 'perfect game' in bowling? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: A score of 300 -- 12 consecutive strikes

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What sport is known as 'The Beautiful Game'? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: Soccer (football) -- Pele popularized the phrase

prompts.text: First to shout it out: In cricket, what is a 'century'? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Scoring 100 runs in a single innings -- a major achievement for a batsman

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What sport uses a shuttlecock? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: Badminton

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the fastest ball sport in the world? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Jai alai (pelota) -- the ball can travel over 180 mph. Badminton shuttlecocks have been hit at 300+ mph.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is March Madness? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: The NCAA Division I Men's Basketball Tournament -- a 68-team single-elimination tournament every March/April

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the Heisman Trophy? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: The award for the most outstanding college football player each year

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What university has won the most NCAA basketball championships? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: UCLA -- 11 national championships (John Wooden won 10 of them)

prompts.text: First to shout it out: In the NFL, what is the 'combine'? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The NFL Scouting Combine -- where college prospects showcase their physical abilities (40-yard dash, bench press, etc.) for NFL teams before the draft

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What video game has the biggest esports prize pool? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Dota 2's The International -- prize pools have exceeded $40 million

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What game is the most popular esport by viewership? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: League of Legends -- the World Championship regularly draws over 100 million viewers

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What doping scandal involved Lance Armstrong? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: He was stripped of 7 Tour de France titles after admitting to systematic doping throughout his career. He confessed on Oprah in 2013.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What infamous incident involved a figure skater's rival getting attacked before the 1994 Olympics? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Tonya Harding's ex-husband arranged for Nancy Kerrigan to be clubbed in the knee. Kerrigan still competed and won silver.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is 'Deflategate'? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Tom Brady and the Patriots were accused of deflating footballs below regulation pressure in the 2014 AFC Championship. Brady was suspended 4 games.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What was the 'Miracle on Ice'? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The 1980 US Olympic hockey team -- a group of college amateurs -- beat the heavily favored Soviet Union team 4-3 during the Cold War

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What soccer player headbutted an opponent in the 2006 World Cup Final and got sent off? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Zinedine Zidane headbutted Marco Materazzi in the chest after Materazzi allegedly insulted his family. France lost on penalties.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What NBA player choked his coach during practice? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Latrell Sprewell choked Golden State Warriors coach P.J. Carlesimo in 1997 -- he was suspended for 68 games

Everyone must flex after every answer for {rule_turns} rounds. No flex = 2 sips.
No saying any sport names for {rule_turns} rounds. Slip up = 2 sips.
{player1} is the Coach. Everyone must get their approval before drinking for {rule_turns} rounds.
Before drinking, you must do a sports celebration (fist pump, spike, etc.) for {rule_turns} rounds. Skip it = extra sip.
For {rule_turns} rounds, everyone must speak like a sports commentator. Normal voice = 2 sips.
prompts.rule_end_text: Commentary is over. Back to normal.

prompts.text: No one can say 'game' for {rule_turns} rounds. Slip up = 2 sips.
{player1} is the Ref. They can call 'foul' on anyone for {rule_turns} rounds -- foul = 1 sip.
{player1} is Thumbmaster. When they put their thumb on the table, last to notice takes 2 sips. {rule_turns} rounds.
No pointing for {rule_turns} rounds. If you point, take 2 sips.
For {rule_turns} rounds, drink with your non-dominant hand. Wrong hand = 2 sips.
prompts.rule_end_text: Drink with whatever hand you want.

prompts.text: {player1} picks a word. For {rule_turns} rounds, whoever says that word takes 2 sips.
For {rule_turns} rounds, before drinking you must say 'And the crowd goes wild!' No cheer = extra sip.
prompts.rule_end_text: The crowd has left. No more cheering required.

prompts.text: For {rule_turns} rounds, anyone who laughs takes 1 sip.
Everyone must high-five someone after drinking for {rule_turns} rounds. No high-five = 1 sip.
For {rule_turns} rounds, everyone must whisper. Full volume = 2 sips.
For {rule_turns} rounds, you must stand up when answering. Stay seated = 2 sips.
Everyone must end sentences with 'and that's why I deserve the MVP' for {rule_turns} rounds. Forget = 2 sips.
For {rule_turns} rounds, you must trash talk the person next to you before drinking. No trash talk = extra sip.
For {rule_turns} rounds, every time someone drinks they must do a sports grunt. No grunt = 1 sip.
For {rule_turns} rounds, the last person to answer any question takes 1 extra sip. Speed matters.
